notes: This file is intentionally empty at launch. Adding attacker-controlled org names here is risky because operators routinely use brand-impersonation typosquats of legitimate projects. Publishing a scam-org identifier publicly can collaterally damage the real legitimate project's reputation through pure name-association.
1. Org must be definitively attacker-controlled (not an impersonated real project).
2. Must have a citable public source (CISA/FBI/Mandiant/Unit 42/Snyk/Socket/Phylum/ESET advisory).
3. Attribution must be explicit in the source.
4. For typosquats: prefer detecting via pattern rules (Levenshtein distance from known legitimate orgs) over naming the specific squat.
Known historical attacker-controlled fronts from public sources (subject to policy above before adding):
- See Mandiant UNC5267 / 'Famous Chollima' writeups for additional persona clusters.

Clonesafe's own sample 001 uses brand impersonation — the attacker-controlled org name is intentionally REDACTED from this repo to avoid harming the impersonated legitimate Web3 project. See samples/contagious-interview-001/README.md for the policy rationale.
